strengths: Rich, detailed examples of ATL skills in action, including authentic email exchanges to demonstrate collaboration and communication
Effective use of self-management and research skills, as shown in your monitoring of progress and application of the CARRDS framework to assess source reliability
Creative application of media literacy and performance skillsâusing survey data to guide design and a dramatic monologue to engage your audience
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You provide extensive, well-supported evidence of how you applied a variety of ATL skills. The inclusion of real artifacts (emails, survey results, digital-tool screenshots) and the clear explanation of how each skill contributed to your productâs development highlight your reflective and strategic use of these skills. No significant gaps were identified in this criterion; continue to leverage concrete examples to illustrate your process.

strengths: Clear, sequenced research plan with stage-by-stage tasks that ensure systematic progress toward the learning goal
Comprehensive timeline and structured plan that outline tasks and deadlines, demonstrating strong organization and prioritization
weaknesses: Success criteria for both the learning goal and the product are described narratively but lack quantifiable, measurable targets
Activities in the timeline are not explicitly mapped back to specific success criteria, making it harder to track which tasks address which goals
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates a solid grasp of sequencing and time management, with a well-structured timeline and clear task breakdown. To elevate this further, you should transform narrative criteria into discrete, measurable targets (for example, âanalyze five case studiesâ or âsurvey ten participantsâ) and include a column in your timeline that links each activity directly to the success criteria it fulfills. This will allow for clearer evaluation of when each part of your goal has been met.

strengths: Insightful reflection on the real-world impact of your product, including social-media metrics and community outreach outcomes
Thoughtful analysis of personal growth, particularly in understanding the challenges of reaching under-educated audiences
Effective linking of peer feedback to decisions about word count and content, showing responsiveness to your audience
weaknesses: The evaluation table against your success criteria is incompleteâseveral criteria (aesthetics, content, target audience, length) lack ratings or supporting evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflections demonstrate depth and self-awareness, especially in evaluating stakeholder impact and your own learning journey. To fully meet the reflecting criterion, ensure that every success criterion is addressed in your evaluation table with clear ratings and specific evidence. Completing this table will strengthen the rigor and completeness of your reflection.

annotations.comment: Your learning goal is clear, but consider adding measurable success criteria (e.g., identify three FGM types, analyze five case studies) so you can evaluate when youâve achieved each aspect of your goal.
Reflecting on social media and community outreach metrics demonstrates awareness of your productâs real-world impact on stakeholders.
Your reflection on the effort needed to reach uneducated audiences shows deep understanding of the personal and community impact of your project.
Including the actual email exchange is an excellent illustration of authentic communication and collaboration to enhance your handbookâs design.

The product success criteria are described narratively but lack discrete, measurable targets. Break them into specific criteria (e.g., include five culturally relevant images, survey ten participants) for clearer evaluation.
This detailed timeline is comprehensive. To strengthen alignment, reference which specific success criteria each activity addresses in a dedicated column.
Your stage-by-stage research plan demonstrates clear sequencing and prioritization, ensuring you gather the evidence needed for your learning goal.
Excellent explanation of how you structured your research and monitored progress using your plan, showing effective application of self-management and research ATL skills.
Your plan section clearly outlines tasks and timelines, showing a structured approach to achieving your product goal.
Your reflective evaluation of research methods is thorough, demonstrating critical analysis of source selection and reliability using the CARRDS criteria.
The CARRDS table provides concrete evidence of how you assessed information credibilityâa strong demonstration of media literacy skills.
Your use of digital tools (calendar app, to-do lists) shows effective organizational skills, keeping you on schedule and supporting sustained progress.
You provide a clear example of communication ATL skills by arranging lessons and translating guidance into product improvementsâwell done.
Survey results are well-presented and directly inform design decisionsâexcellent use of research ATL skills to tailor your product.
Your explanation of audience research (media literacy) and how it guided word count and design shows strong use of data-driven decision-making skills.
Applying performance and communication skills through a dramatic monologue shows creative engagement with your audience and strengthens your advocacy.
The evaluation table against success criteria begins well but remains incomplete. Ensure all criteria (aesthetics, content, target audience, length) have ratings and supporting evidence.
Your content evaluation shows comprehensive coverage of subtopics aligned with your goalsâstrong evidence of thorough product development.
Linking peer feedback to your final word count demonstrates effective reflection on audience preferences and achievement of length criteria.
